title: Create Formam package and storage module scaffolding
goal: Introduce a new package boundary that will own Formam path, schema, and persistence decisions.
context: Wave A corresponds to tasks 2-7 in AZQ_BUILD_TASKS_STAGE_2.md.
The live repository layout is under azq/, not src/azq/.
Stage 2 is about introducing deliverables and maps without leaking into Agenda task work.
requirements: Create the azq/formam package.
Create azq/formam/storage.py.
Do not wire CLI commands yet.
Keep this commit limited to Formam scaffolding and import-safe module boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: The new Formam storage module can be imported without error.

title: Add canonical Formam path constants and directory helpers
goal: Centralize path ownership for Formam deliverable and map persistence.
context: The canonical Stage 2 storage locations are data/form/deliverables/ and data/form/maps/.
One module should own all Formam path decisions.
Goal maps should use a deterministic filename convention keyed by goal_id.
requirements: Define constants for the deliverables directory and maps directory.
Add helpers to ensure both directories exist.
Add a helper to map a deliverable_id like DELIV_001 to a markdown file path in data/form/deliverables/.
Add a helper to map a goal_id like FINIS_001 to a map file path such as GOAL_FINIS_001_MAP.md.
Add a helper to list deliverable files in deterministic order.
acceptance_criteria: All Formam storage paths are defined in azq/formam/storage.py.
The Formam directories can be created on demand.
Deliverable and map path lookup is deterministic.
suggested_commit_message: add formam storage path constants and helpers

title: Define the canonical normalized deliverable schema
goal: Create a normalization function that converts partial deliverable-shaped data into the Stage 2 canonical schema.
context: Canonical Stage 2 deliverable fields are: deliverable_id, goal_id, title, artifact_description, dependencies, status, created.
Stage 2 starts with stub deliverables, so partial generated records are expected.
The schema must stay focused on artifact definition and boundaries of work rather than task lists.
requirements: Add a normalization helper that accepts a partial deliverable dict and returns a canonical deliverable dict.
Provide conservative fallback values for missing artifact_description, dependencies, status, and created fields.
Preserve given values where possible rather than silently inventing structure.
Do not read or write files yet in this task beyond code changes.
acceptance_criteria: One function returns a complete canonical deliverable record.
The canonical output always includes deliverable_id, goal_id, title, artifact_description, dependencies, status, and created.
Dependencies always normalize to a list.

title: Implement markdown deliverable file serialization and parsing
goal: Make canonical deliverable records readable and writable as diff-friendly markdown files.
context: Stage 2 requires one deliverable per file named DELIV_###.md.
The file format should remain simple enough for later Agenda code to read without transitional glue.
Human readability matters because Formam is a planning surface, not hidden state.
requirements: Choose and implement a markdown on-disk format for deliverable files.
Add serializer and parser helpers in azq/formam/storage.py.
Ensure all canonical fields are preserved round-trip.
Keep the format simple and explicit.
acceptance_criteria: A canonical deliverable record can be serialized to markdown and parsed back without losing required fields.
The resulting file is human-readable and diff-friendly.
suggested_commit_message: add markdown deliverable file read write format

title: Implement file-backed deliverable repository reads and id allocation
goal: Allow deliverable storage alone to support listing, lookup, goal grouping, and next-id generation.
context: Later command work will depend on storage.py for all deliverable reads.
Lookup should be exact-match on deliverable_id and goal_id.
ID allocation must remain stable and deterministic.
requirements: Add functions to load all deliverables from data/form/deliverables/.
Add a function to load one deliverable by exact deliverable_id.
Add a function to load deliverables for one exact goal_id.
Add a function to compute the next DELIV_### id from existing deliverable files.
Keep ordering deterministic.
acceptance_criteria: File storage alone can list deliverables, load one deliverable, group deliverables by goal, and generate the next stable deliverable id.
suggested_commit_message: add file backed formam deliverable reads and id allocation

title: Add canonical goal-parent validation helpers
goal: Ensure Formam can prove a deliverable descends from a real canonical goal before any deliverable file is written.
context: Stage 2 requires every deliverable to point back to exactly one goal initially.
Formam should not create deliverables for missing parent goals.
If active-only validation is enforced, that rule should be explicit and inspectable.
requirements: Add a helper that validates a canonical goal by exact goal_id using the Stage 1 Finis storage layer.
Reject missing parent goals before any Formam write occurs.
Keep goal validation logic in the Formam storage boundary rather than scattering it across future command modules.
Return or raise enough information that a future command can print a readable error.
acceptance_criteria: Formam exposes a goal validation helper.
The helper checks parent goals by exact goal_id.
A future build command can use the helper to stop invalid deliverable creation early.
suggested_commit_message: add formam canonical goal validation helpers
